# agent-architecture.history

This history documents Kuro's evolution from performative skepticism to actionable verification: replacing passive waiting with active state confirmation through grep, stat, and inbox dedup checks. Key lesson: memory entries capture past state but decay with time; actions must begin with re-verification of current artifact status rather than continuing deductions from stale snapshots. The pattern emerges across three debug cycles where falsifiers revealed working systems (HN pipeline, budget tracking) that had been misdiagnosed as broken solely because assumptions relied on outdated memory entries.
